records.ety: [F. ombre a shade, L. umbra; cf. F. ombrelle a sunshade, OF. also ombrire. See Umbrella.]

defs: In ancient armor, a visor, or projection like the peak of a cap, to which a face guard was sometimes attached. This was sometimes fixed, and sometimes moved freely upon the helmet and could be raised like the beaver. Called also umber, and umbril.
quotes: But only vented up her umbriere.
